title: binary_to_term decoder — ETF to runtime Terms on process heap
purpose: beamr has an ETF decoder in the loader pipeline (loader/decode/etf.rs) that parses ETF-encoded literals from BEAM files into a Literal enum, which is then materialized into heap terms by the constant pool. This decoder is loader-specific: it reads from a BoundedCursor, produces intermediate Literal values, and allocates on a module-level constant pool — not a process heap. The erlang:binary_to_term/1 BIF needs a different path: take an arbitrary binary term at runtime, parse the ETF bytes, and allocate the resulting term directly on the calling process's heap. This is the runtime decoder, complementing B-086's encoder to close the serialization round-trip. The runtime decoder writes Terms directly to the process heap (using existing boxed writers — write_tuple, write_cons, write_float, etc.) without the intermediate Literal representation. This avoids double allocation and is the standard approach for runtime deserialization.
task: Implement a runtime ETF decoder with a decode budget (depth, heap, atom limits), a core decode_term function that reads ETF bytes and allocates Terms directly on the process heap for all 14+ tag types, and register erlang:binary_to_term/1 as a BIF wired to the decoder.

requirements.title: Runtime decode budget
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a RuntimeDecodeBudget struct in etf/decode.rs with fields max_depth (default 256), max_heap_words (default remaining heap capacity of the calling process), current_depth, and heap_words_used. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide descend()/ascend() methods for depth tracking that return an error on depth exceeded. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a charge_heap(words: usize) method that returns an error on budget exceeded. THE SYSTEM SHALL define a DecodeError type with variants DepthExceeded, HeapBudgetExceeded, InvalidTag(u8), Truncated, InvalidAtom, and InvalidUtf8.
acceptance: etf/decode.rs with RuntimeDecodeBudget struct
max_depth: usize — maximum nesting depth (default 256, matching loader)
max_heap_words: usize — maximum heap words to allocate (default: remaining heap capacity of the calling process)
current_depth: usize — tracks current recursion depth
heap_words_used: usize — tracks allocations
descend() / ascend() methods for depth tracking, return error on depth exceeded
charge_heap(words: usize) method, return error on budget exceeded
Error type: DecodeError with variants DepthExceeded, HeapBudgetExceeded, InvalidTag(u8), Truncated, InvalidAtom, InvalidUtf8

requirements.title: Core runtime decoder — decode_term function
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a pub fn decode_term(bytes: &[u8], process: &mut Process, atom_table: &AtomTable) -> Result<Term, DecodeError> that validates the version byte (131) and dispatches on ETF tags. WHEN decoding SMALL_INTEGER_EXT (97), THE SYSTEM SHALL return Term::small_int(value). WHEN decoding INTEGER_EXT (98), THE SYSTEM SHALL return Term::small_int(i32 as i64). WHEN decoding NEW_FLOAT_EXT (70), THE SYSTEM SHALL call write_float(heap, f64). WHEN decoding ATOM_UTF8_EXT (118), SMALL_ATOM_UTF8_EXT (119), or ATOM_EXT (100), THE SYSTEM SHALL intern the name via atom_table and return Term::atom. WHEN decoding SMALL_TUPLE_EXT (104) or LARGE_TUPLE_EXT (105), THE SYSTEM SHALL recursively decode elements and call write_tuple. WHEN decoding NIL_EXT (106), THE SYSTEM SHALL return Term::NIL. WHEN decoding STRING_EXT (107), THE SYSTEM SHALL build a list of small integers on heap via cons cells. WHEN decoding LIST_EXT (108), THE SYSTEM SHALL recursively decode elements and tail and build a cons list on heap. WHEN decoding BINARY_EXT (109), THE SYSTEM SHALL call write_binary. WHEN decoding SMALL_BIG_EXT (110) or LARGE_BIG_EXT (111), THE SYSTEM SHALL call write_bigint or return Term::small_int if the value fits. WHEN decoding MAP_EXT (116), THE SYSTEM SHALL decode key-value pairs and call write_map. WHEN decoding EXPORT_EXT (113), THE SYSTEM SHALL decode module/function/arity atoms and build a tuple. WHEN decoding NEW_PID_EXT (88) or PID_EXT (103), THE SYSTEM SHALL return Term::pid. WHEN decoding NEWER_REFERENCE_EXT (90) or REFERENCE_EXT (114), THE SYSTEM SHALL call write_reference. THE SYSTEM SHALL charge heap and descend/ascend for each recursive allocation.
acceptance: pub fn decode_term(bytes: &[u8], process: &mut Process, atom_table: &AtomTable) -> Result<Term, DecodeError>
Validates version byte (131) at the start
SMALL_INTEGER_EXT (97) -> Term::small_int(value)
INTEGER_EXT (98) -> Term::small_int(i32 as i64)
NEW_FLOAT_EXT (70) -> write_float(heap, f64) -> boxed Term
ATOM_UTF8_EXT (118), SMALL_ATOM_UTF8_EXT (119), ATOM_EXT (100) -> atom_table.intern(name) -> Term::atom(atom)
SMALL_TUPLE_EXT (104), LARGE_TUPLE_EXT (105) -> recursively decode elements, write_tuple(heap, &elements)
NIL_EXT (106) -> Term::NIL
STRING_EXT (107) -> build list of small integers (byte values) on heap via cons cells
LIST_EXT (108) -> recursively decode elements and tail, build cons list on heap
BINARY_EXT (109) -> write_binary(heap, &bytes)
SMALL_BIG_EXT (110), LARGE_BIG_EXT (111) -> write_bigint(heap, sign, &limbs) or Term::small_int() if fits
MAP_EXT (116) -> decode key-value pairs, write_map(heap, &keys, &values)
EXPORT_EXT (113) -> decode module/function/arity atoms, build {Module, Function, Arity} tuple
NEW_PID_EXT (88), PID_EXT (103) -> Term::pid(id)
NEWER_REFERENCE_EXT (90), REFERENCE_EXT (114) -> write_reference(heap, id)
Budget checks: charge heap for each allocation, descend/ascend for recursion
Test: decode [131, 97, 42] -> Term::small_int(42)
Test: decode encoded tuple {ok, 42} -> 2-tuple on heap
Test: decode encoded list [1, 2, 3] -> cons list on heap
Test: round-trip: encode a term with B-086's encoder, decode it back, verify equality

requirements.title: erlang:binary_to_term/1 BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:binary_to_term/1 is called with a binary term, THE SYSTEM SHALL extract bytes via Binary::new(arg)?.as_bytes(), call etf::decode::decode_term(bytes, process, atom_table), and return the decoded term. WHEN the argument is not a binary, THE SYSTEM SHALL return ExecError::Badarg. WHEN decoding fails with any DecodeError variant, THE SYSTEM SHALL return ExecError::Badarg.
acceptance: erlang:binary_to_term/1 registered in BIF registry
Takes a binary term as argument
Extracts bytes via Binary::new(arg)?.as_bytes()
Calls etf::decode::decode_term(bytes, process, atom_table)
Returns the decoded term
On non-binary argument, returns ExecError::Badarg
On DecodeError::*, returns ExecError::Badarg (matching BEAM semantics — invalid ETF is a badarg)
Test: binary_to_term(term_to_binary(X)) round-trips for atoms, integers, tuples, lists, maps, binaries

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement safe mode or options (B-088)
SHALL NOT reuse the loader's Literal-based decode path (different allocation strategy)
verification: cargo test passes with all existing tests plus new ETF decoder tests
RuntimeDecodeBudget enforces depth and heap limits
decode_term handles all 14+ ETF tag types correctly
erlang:binary_to_term/1 BIF is registered and returns correct terms
Round-trip: encode then decode recovers the original term for all supported types
